title: megalastrum praetermissum
intro: Megalastrum praetermissum is a species of fern in the family Ophioglossaceae, commonly known as the 'adder-fern' or 'adder's tongue fern'. This plant is native to tropical regions and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots and rhizomes of Megalastrum praetermissum contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as fever, pain, and infections. The herbal preparations of Megalastrum praetermissum include infusions and decoctions made from the roots and rhizomes, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
